 {"id":"bd-1ce6","title":"EPIC: Agent-Friendliness Gap Closure - Tool Suite Maturity","description":"# EPIC: Agent-Friendliness Gap Closure - Tool Suite Maturity

## Overview
This epic tracks closing all agent-friendliness gaps identified during the 2026-01-25 re-underwriting audit. Ten tools scored below 5.0/5 and require improvements to reach full maturity.

## Background
The Dicklesworthstone tool suite aims to be exceptionally AI-agent-friendly with:
- Consistent `--format json|toon` flags
- `*_OUTPUT_FORMAT` environment variables
- JSON envelope with stable structure
- Schema export via `--schema` or `schema` subcommands
- Comprehensive AGENTS.md documentation
- Error codes with hints

## Tools Requiring Work (by priority)

### Priority 1 (Score ≤4.3)
| Tool | Score | Critical Gaps |
|------|-------|---------------|
| dcg (destructive_command_guard) | 4.0/5 | Missing AGENTS.md, no TOON, fake SARIF |
| slb (simultaneous_launch_button) | 7/10 | -t flag panic, Go map output bug |
| rch (remote_compilation_helper) | 4.3/5 | No schema, no error registry |

### Priority 2 (Score 4.7)
| Tool | Score | Key Gaps |
|------|-------|----------|
| xf | 4.7/5 | No TOON, no streaming |
| ms (meta_skill) | 4.7/5 | Binary rebuild, no schema |
| pt (process_triage) | 4.7/5 | No QUICKSTART, no schema |
| mcp_agent_mail | 4.7/5 | Docs fixes, test fixtures |

### Priority 3 (Score 4.8)
| Tool | Score | Key Gaps |
|------|-------|----------|
| cm (cass_memory_system) | 4.8/5 | No --format flag, no TOON |
| caam (coding_agent_account_manager) | 4.8/5 | No --format flag, no TOON |
| ubs (ultimate_bug_scanner) | 4.8/5 | No TOON, no env var |

## Success Criteria
- All 10 tools score 5.0/5 on agent-friendliness
- Consistent patterns across the entire suite
- Full TOON integration where applicable
- Schema export for all machine outputs
- No critical bugs blocking agent usage

## Methodology
Each tool gets a sub-epic with granular tasks. Dependencies ensure:
1. Bug fixes before new features
2. Core infrastructure (AGENTS.md) before enhancements
3. Schema work after format flags
4. Documentation updates last

## Related Work
- AGENT_FRIENDLINESS_REPORT.md files created for each tool
- toon_rust crate provides TOON serialization
- OutputFormat enum pattern from beads_rust/repo_updater","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T18:31:31.139406304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:32:59.557904160Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:32:59.557567335Z","close_reason":"OUT OF SCOPE: This epic tracks agent-friendliness improvements for OTHER tools (dcg, slb, rch, xf, ms, pt, cm, caam, ubs, mcp_agent_mail) NOT wa. These improvements should be tracked in those respective projects, not in wa's backlog.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1dnn","title":"Add wa robot why command","description":"## Goal
Add `wa robot why <code>` command for agents to understand error codes and policy denials.

## Rationale
When agents receive errors like `robot.policy_denied` or `robot.require_approval`, they need programmatic access to explanations and remediation steps.

## Implementation
1. Add `RobotCommands::Why { code: String }`
2. Query `wa_core::explanations::get_explanation(code)`
3. Return JSON with: scenario, brief, detailed, suggestions, see_also
4. Create wa-robot-why.json schema

## Response Format
\`\`\`json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "code": "deny.alt_screen",
    "scenario": "Send denied because alt-screen is active",
    "brief": "Pane is in full-screen mode (vim, less, etc.)",
    "detailed": "The pane is currently displaying...",
    "suggestions": ["Exit the full-screen application first", ...],
    "see_also": ["wa policy", "wa status --pane <id>"]
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot why deny.alt_screen` returns explanation
- `wa robot why unknown.code` returns error with available codes
- JSON schema validates response","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:14.633050084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:04.476889868Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:04.476841497Z","close_reason":"Implemented: wa robot why command using get_explanation()","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dnn","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ey4","title":"Add wa robot workflow abort command","description":"# wa robot workflow abort

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-workflow-abort.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Implement workflow abort with cleanup and audit trail.

## Implementation
1. Accept execution_id and optional --reason
2. Validate workflow is abortable (not already completed)
3. Mark as aborting, run cleanup steps
4. Release pane lock
5. Record in audit trail

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Abort stops running workflow
- Unit: Cleanup steps run (unless --force)
- Unit: Pane lock released
- Unit: Error code stability (E_ALREADY_COMPLETED, etc.)
- E2E: See bd-qvbz for lifecycle scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Safely aborts running workflows
- [ ] Cleanup steps execute (unless --force)
- [ ] Pane lock released after abort
- [ ] Audit trail records abort with reason
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

+{"id":"bd-1ez8","title":"Set up automated nightly rebase CI for WezTerm fork","description":"## Overview

WezTerm is updated nightly by its author. To maintain our feature branch, we need automated rebasing with conflict detection and alerting.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-20fw (WezTerm fork with wa integration)

## CI Workflow Design

### GitHub Actions Workflow

\`\`\`.github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml\`\`\`
\`\`\`yaml
name: Rebase on Upstream WezTerm

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * *'  # Daily at 6 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:      # Manual trigger

jobs:
  rebase:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: feature/wa-integration
          token: \${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "wa-bot"
          git config user.email "wa-bot@example.com"
      
      - name: Add Upstream Remote
        run: |
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
          git fetch upstream main
      
      - name: Attempt Rebase
        id: rebase
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          git rebase upstream/main
          echo "rebase_status=\$?" >> \$GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Push if Successful
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          git push --force-with-lease origin feature/wa-integration
      
      - name: Create Conflict Issue
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'failure'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { data: issues } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              labels: 'rebase-conflict',
              state: 'open'
            });
            
            if (issues.length === 0) {
              await github.rest.issues.create({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                title: 'Rebase conflict with upstream WezTerm',
                body: \`The nightly rebase on upstream WezTerm failed due to conflicts.
                
                **Action Required**: Manual resolution needed.
                
                \\\`\\\`\\\`bash
                git fetch upstream main
                git rebase upstream/main
                # Resolve conflicts
                git rebase --continue
                git push --force-with-lease
                \\\`\\\`\\\`
                \`,
                labels: ['rebase-conflict', 'urgent']
              });
            }
      
      - name: Run Tests After Rebase
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          cargo build --features wa-integration
          cargo test --features wa-integration

  notify:
    needs: rebase
    if: failure()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send Notification
        run: |
          # Notify via email, Slack, Discord, etc.
          echo "Rebase failed - notification would be sent"
\`\`\`

### Handling Conflicts

When conflicts occur:
1. CI creates GitHub issue with "rebase-conflict" label
2. Human/agent reviews conflicting files
3. Usually conflicts are in files we modified (minimal set)
4. Resolve, test, push

### Minimizing Conflicts

To reduce conflict frequency:
1. Keep wa changes behind \`#[cfg(feature)]\` — no logic changes to upstream code
2. Use separate files where possible (mux/src/wa_events.rs)
3. Add wa code at end of functions, not middle
4. Avoid reformatting upstream code

## Alternative: Patch-Based Approach

Instead of maintaining a branch, maintain a patch series:

\`\`\`bash
# Generate patches
git format-patch upstream/main..feature/wa-integration -o patches/

# Apply patches to fresh clone
git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
cd wezterm
git am ../patches/*.patch
\`\`\`

Patches are more explicit about what we change, easier to review.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow created
- [ ] Nightly rebase runs automatically
- [ ] Conflicts create GitHub issues
- [ ] Notifications configured
- [ ] Post-rebase tests run
- [ ] Documentation for manual conflict resolution
- [ ] 30-day trial period: track conflict frequency

## Files to Create

- .github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml
- docs/maintaining-wezterm-fork.md

## References

- GitHub Actions: https://docs.github.com/en/actions
- Git rebase: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-rebase
- WezTerm release cadence: nightly builds","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:53.398103664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:50:03.655977304Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ez8","depends_on_id":"bd-20fw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:50:03.655935456Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ez8","depends_on_id":"bd-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:49:53.407112375Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1zgb","title":"E2E: reliability hardening scenarios with verbose logs","description":"## Goal
Validate resilience features end-to-end with detailed logging and artifacts.

## Requirements
- Execute fault injection scenarios that trigger circuit breaker, retry, and graceful degradation paths.
- Capture verbose logs (trace/debug) plus key metrics snapshots as artifacts.
- Verify user-facing output is stable and actionable during degraded mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scenarios pass locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include full logs, triggered fault labels, and observed policy outputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:24.619323361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:47.826611461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"wa-37x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-20fw","title":"Create WezTerm fork with wa feature flag and minimal integration points","description":"## Overview

Fork WezTerm and add the minimal code needed to support wa event delivery. All changes are behind \`#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]\`.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-3gon (WaEventSink trait design)

## Repository Setup

### 1. Fork WezTerm
\`\`\`bash
gh repo fork wez/wezterm --clone
cd wezterm
git checkout -b feature/wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Add Feature Flag
In Cargo.toml (workspace root):
\`\`\`toml
[workspace.features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

In relevant crates (mux/Cargo.toml, wezterm/Cargo.toml):
\`\`\`toml
[features]
wa-integration = []
\`\`\`

## Modification Points

### 1. mux/src/lib.rs — Event Sink Registration

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
mod wa_events;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
use wa_events::WaEventSink;

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
static WA_EVENT_SINK: OnceCell<Arc<dyn WaEventSink>> = OnceCell::new();

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub fn register_wa_event_sink(sink: Arc<dyn WaEventSink>) {
    WA_EVENT_SINK.set(sink).ok();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
pub(crate) fn emit_wa_event(event: impl FnOnce(&dyn WaEventSink)) {
    if let Some(sink) = WA_EVENT_SINK.get() {
        event(sink.as_ref());
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit Output Events

In the method that receives PTY output:
\`\`\`rust
fn process_pty_output(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
    // Existing WezTerm code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_output(self.pane_id, data);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 3. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit State Changes

In methods that update pane state:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_title(&mut self, title: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    self.emit_state_change();
}

#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn emit_state_change(&self) {
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_pane_state_change(self.pane_id, &self.get_wa_state());
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 4. mux/src/pane.rs — Emit User-Var Changes

In the OSC 1337 handler:
\`\`\`rust
fn set_user_var(&mut self, name: String, value: String) {
    // Existing code...
    
    #[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
    crate::emit_wa_event(|sink| {
        sink.on_user_var_changed(self.pane_id, &name, &value);
    });
}
\`\`\`

### 5. wezterm/src/main.rs — Initialize Sink

\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(feature = "wa-integration")]
fn init_wa_integration() {
    if let Ok(socket_path) = std::env::var("WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET") {
        match wa_socket_sink::connect(&socket_path) {
            Ok(sink) => mux::register_wa_event_sink(sink),
            Err(e) => log::warn!("Failed to connect to wa socket: {}", e),
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Testing the Fork

### 1. Build with Feature
\`\`\`bash
cargo build --features wa-integration
\`\`\`

### 2. Verify Default Build Unaffected
\`\`\`bash
cargo build  # Should work identically to upstream
\`\`\`

### 3. Integration Test
\`\`\`bash
# Start wa watch (which creates the socket)
wa watch &

# Start WezTerm with wa integration
WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET=/tmp/wa/events.sock ./target/debug/wezterm

# Verify events received
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WezTerm fork created with feature/wa-integration branch
- [ ] Feature flag added to workspace
- [ ] Event emission added to pane output path
- [ ] Event emission added to state change paths
- [ ] Event emission added to user-var handler
- [ ] Default build (no feature) compiles and runs unchanged
- [ ] Feature build compiles and runs
- [ ] Events actually received by wa (integration test)

## Risks

- WezTerm internal APIs may change — minimize touchpoints
- Performance regression — benchmark before/after
- Thread safety bugs — careful with event emission

## Files to Modify (in WezTerm fork)

- Cargo.toml (workspace) — add feature
- mux/Cargo.toml — add feature
- mux/src/lib.rs — event sink registration
- mux/src/pane.rs — event emission
- wezterm/Cargo.toml — add feature
- wezterm/src/main.rs — initialization
- NEW: mux/src/wa_events.rs — trait + socket sink implementation

## References

- WezTerm mux: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux

 {"id":"bd-2kl8","title":"Change wa robot workflow to require explicit 'run' subcommand","description":"## Goal
Change `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id>` to `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane_id>` for consistency with planned workflow list/status/abort subcommands.

## Breaking Change
This is a breaking change. Agents using `wa robot workflow` must update to `wa robot workflow run`.

## Implementation
1. Add `WorkflowSubcommand` enum with `Run`, `List`, `Status`, `Abort` variants
2. Change `RobotCommands::Workflow` to take subcommand instead of positional args
3. Update quick-start guide examples
4. Update error messages to reference new syntax

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane_id>` works as before
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id>` shows helpful error pointing to new syntax
- Quick-start guide reflects new command structure","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:08.619598006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:54.068422868Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:54.068365820Z","close_reason":"Implemented: workflow run verb change with subcommand pattern","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kl8","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:09:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
